FY.executive_summary: UnitedHealth Group Incorporated's (UNH) latest fiscal year report reveals a mixed bag of results. Revenue surged 11.8% to $447.6B, however, this growth did not translate to the bottom line, as net income declined 16.3% to $12.1B, resulting in a 25.1% decrease in net margin to 2.7%. This decline in profitability is further exacerbated by a 17.6% decrease in return on equity (ROE) to 12.8%. Meanwhile, the company's balance sheet health is a concern, as debt-to-equity increased by 4.6% to 2.21x, while the current ratio decreased by 4.6% to 0.79. Despite this, the stock has seen a significant surge, closing at $424.62, a 40.3% increase over the past three months. Overall, UNH's financial health remains a mixed bag, with revenue growth not being enough to offset declines in profitability and balance sheet health.
